title: Feature Request: markdown or code formatting in Updates
excerpt: I run a web development shop and just started using Monday this week. When I’m posting an Update, I would really like the option to use Markdown formatting. This would be useful for a number of reasons, but here’s one ex…

posts.author: diegovogel (Diego Vogel)
posts.cookedHtml:
I run a web development shop and just started using Monday this week. When I’m posting an Update, I would really like the option to use Markdown formatting. This would be useful for a number of reasons, but here’s one example. My team and I often share code snippets and these get posted in Updates. When we put code in an Update, the tab spacing gets stripped. Markdown would totally solve this with the back ticks for code formatting.
Alternatively, adding some code formatting buttons to the editor would work too (code block and inline code). But I think allowing markdown would be more flexible.

posts.author: willkessler (Will Kessler)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1000 !!!

Dev’s using Monday hate it because , no markdown support. Please add.

posts.author: RonTuretzky (Ron Turetzky)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ugh yea I feel you guys for sure
Quick wordaround is to CNTRL + ALT +SHIFT+C in slack and then copy and paste into the update

for some reason that makes monday recognize it as a code block

proof:

image813×226 2.89 KB

posts.author: kcdyer (kc)
posts.cookedHtml: 1000% - This is turning into a deal breaker for us with monday FAST. We would LOVE a code block or code snippet feature.

posts.author: ddbradshaw (Danny Bradshaw)
posts.cookedHtml: Markdown exists in the community editor for Monday, just not Monday itself

posts.author: mfan (michael fanuzzi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Indeed. This community section is built on Discourse, which has supported this from the start.

But this is really becoming a difficult thing for my team. We’re an agency and (like many) development is a large part of what we do. Even non-developers are used to using `backticks` in-line for formatting thanks to Slack, which has also supported this since day one. We sometimes even just use backticks anyway for our own clarity of communication, even though they do nothing in Monday.

Being able to do this in blocks with the standard MD triple-backticks would be pretty nice too.

I think language-specific formatting would be a stretch goal and I wouldn’t let it hold up getting this very important feature out. Just simply having the generic block-formatting would be a huge improvement.

posts.author: dominicpixie (Dominic)
posts.cookedHtml:
diegovogel:

ust started using Monday this week. When I’m posting an Update, I would really like the option to use Markdown formatting. This would be useful for a number of reasons, but here’s one example. My team and I often share code snippets and these get posted in Updates. When we put code in an Update, the tab spacing gets strippe

Application called Teamwork supports markdown.

After switching to Monday I really miss it.

Markdown would be an amazing feature.

posts.author: gemma.down (Gemma)
posts.cookedHtml:
100% yes - this is one of my biggest bug bears about using Monday is how clunky the update editor is. As someone mentioned above, even just basic formatting using simplistic markdown would be excellent (i.e. doesn’t have to include syntax highlighting), as well as the ability to quickly create a checklist using the - [ ] notation.

This would also make it much easier to use automations and format things like the updates nicely, and dramatically improve the GitHub integration.

posts.author: RealDogDad (Brandan Foster)
posts.cookedHtml: Second this, I’d love to do the bulk of my collaboration in Monday.com, but no markdown makes it difficult and I wander to other services…

posts.author: TomS (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: Wish I can upvote this many more times. We have both Data Scientists and Engineers on our team. Both use Markdown heavily. We use it to “groom” tasks in an organizational manner. We also use it to write up reports as updates in the tasks. Really wish this is an option!

posts.author: eric.lo (Eric Lo)
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to use markdown for the email integration. Without it, emails sent through monday.com tend to look very awkward. Would love to see markdown rolled out to monday.com.

posts.author: acadama123 (Duong Duy Nguyen)
posts.cookedHtml: Second this! I love formatting my status updates so that it’s easy for others to read and look clean. What monday.com has right now in the Update editor is rather limited.

posts.author: LADK (Leila AIT KACI)
posts.cookedHtml: We would also love to have both markdown and even more code view ! Especially as the inline editor sometimes get crazy when adding / deleting bullet points. It would be very very helpful to be able to cleanup the entry in html directly instead of using back and forth text copies to other softwares (mainly Notepad in this case) to simplify the underneath html.

posts.author: itspjax (Pete)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is Monday’s biggest downside. The text editor has numerous problems, breaks many standard text editing UX conventions (mac / linux shortcuts are treated as if we’re on Windows). It’s tedious, frustrating, and ineffective to use. A simple markdown editor would be wonderful, even just as a new widget we could add separately so you don’t have to migrate anything. There are plenty of great, high quality open source options for this and it really seems like a low effort and super high value task.

I’m close to switching several large organizations from Monday due almost entirely to this feature. We end up writing our descriptions in other apps and pasting into Monday it’s that bad. Please, please fix this.

You have a fantastic product otherwise and this is glaringly out of sync with the quality and thoughtfulness put into pretty much everything else, to the point that it detracts from the perception of everything.

posts.author: Livni (Jonathan Livni)
posts.cookedHtml:
'nuf said, +1
(I’m writing a bit more because the minimum number of characters to submit is 20)

posts.author: rmirabelle (Robert Mirabelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
Code formatting a paragraph is supported, but this is NOT as useful as simple markdown support, because it applies to a whole paragraph only. The more common approach is to highlight individual words within a paragraph.

PLEASE grant us markdown support!

posts.author: andres.olave (Andres Olave)
posts.cookedHtml: We have our 30 person engineering team on JIRA. The only way we can migrate them to monday is if markdown support is added…

posts.author: vicary (vicary)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this feature in the upcoming roadmaps?

posts.author: Sachi (Sachi Law)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 The ability to use Markdown is a must for our team and development workflow.

posts.author: austecon (austecon)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 I mostly want a markdown option in Work Docs. I should be able to just copy-paste an entire markdown file into there and have it render correctly.

If monday.com had this, I would use it to organize everything. I am a software engineer. Full compatibility with markdown is an essential requirement. Cannot live without it.

posts.author: Horizonsong (Karo Zieminski)
posts.cookedHtml: We need this SOOOOOOOO bad!

posts.author: solidjohn (John)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday.
Please just stop what you’re doing - all of you - and have a good long chat about how best to integrate markdown with updates/replies. Then, when you’ve worked it out (shouldn’t be too difficult, since everyone else is doing it) go build it, test it, make it live, then get back to whatever you were doing before.
Thus everyone else in the world can get on with their jobs without having to invent ridiculous work-arounds, and you can bask in your own greatness as you notice your customer satisfaction ratings suddenly rise.
Thanks. You’re amazing!
xJ

posts.author: davidsurver (David)
posts.cookedHtml: Is this feature ever coming? Seems like a must if you want to use Monday as a tech company…

posts.author: sensaria.etaylor (sensaria.etaylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday team, please consider this. Especially now that you’ve got “Monday Dev” as a product. If you’re going all in on dev teams; this is something we would all need/appreciate.

Some of the key points to me:

  • Type backticks before/after something to automatically convert it to inline code formatting. There doesn’t seem to be any inline code format at all yet. Even this by itself as a shortcut/format option would be an improvement without full markdown support.
  • Type three backticks on a new line to start a code block
  • Refresh the formatting on code blocks in general. Courier is ugly/outdated. Something like Source Code Pro or Roboto Mono would be an improvement.
  • Within a multi-line code block, support syntax highlighting for common languages like json, html, css, javascript, sql, c#, etc.
  • Support list and checkbox syntax. This meshes well with your existing formatting options of bullets/numbers and checkboxes.

Thank you for your consideration!

posts.author: musicforte (Greg Percifield)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is really needed. Often, I find myself needing to quote specific parts of someone’s earlier comment, and there isn’t a very attractive way for drawing attention to that, makes it clear that we are quoting someone.

I want to be able to do this:

Markdown would totally solve this with the back ticks for code formatting.
Alternatively, adding some code formatting buttons to the editor would work too (code block and inline code). But I think allowing markdown would be more flexible.

posts.author: nicdev (Nic)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there anything I can do to have Product Management decide to implement this? It’s ridiculous that it doesn’t exist. I spent 10 minutes trying to figure out what I was missing because I was sure Monday had a way to show code as code. After I gave up I landed here and it’s sorely disappointing.

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: this looks great, but, is there any way to query from this view using the api so I can use the data automatically? I’m trying to publish the data to a website and have users edit it on this item view.

posts.author: willt (WillT)
posts.cookedHtml: this is mission critical if monday.com is going to be viable for tech projects imo

posts.author: somidevmark (Mark Cervantes)
posts.cookedHtml: 4 years since the request, still no updates for this fundamental formatting feature…

posts.author: LF_RPS (Luke)
posts.cookedHtml:
I wanted to echo the request for Markdown support in the “Updates” section—this feature would be incredibly valuable, particularly for those of us managing tech teams and projects in Monday.com.

As a tech company, effective communication and clarity are paramount. When sharing detailed technical updates, release notes, or project progress, Markdown allows us to:

  1. Format and organize content efficiently—code snippets, bullet points, headers, and links become much clearer, improving readability for all stakeholders.
  2. Present code or technical details clearly—whether it’s inline code, code blocks, or error logs, Markdown is essential for accurately conveying technical information.
  3. Increase collaboration—with Markdown, it’s easier to include rich content like links to resources, diagrams, and task updates that need a more structured approach than plain text.

Without this feature, we’re forced to work around limitations, which ultimately hampers productivity and the quality of communication within our team and with our clients.

I strongly urge the Monday.com team to prioritize this request. It’s a small change with a big impact, particularly for those of us who rely on clear, well-structured documentation to keep our projects on track.
